items.headline: City commissions outline priorities for next fiscal year
items.summary: Saratoga's five commissions—covering heritage, libraries, parks, traffic safety, and planning—presented their work plans for fiscal year 2026-27. The council approved these plans, which will guide major city initiatives like park improvements, traffic safety projects, and public art installations over the coming year.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is rolling out an amnesty program to help businesses comply with licensing requirements, along with outreach to shopping centers and incentives for retail and startups. This could affect local business owners who may have fallen behind on compliance or are considering opening a shop in Cupertino.

items.headline: Capitol Dental Office opens on North Capitol Avenue
items.summary: A $500,000 dental office is opening at 585 N Capitol Av, bringing new healthcare services to the area. This is the only significant commercial project in this permit batch.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pickup designated as annual city event
items.summary: The City Council approved the Great American Litter Pickup as an official annual city-sponsored special event, authorizing city funds and donations for materials and services. This formalizes a community cleanup effort as a regular San José initiative.

items.headline: Federal housing grants available for community development
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding applications on March 25. These federal funds support affordable housing and community development projects that directly benefit residents.

items.headline: New retail vitality policies could reshape Palo Alto's commercial zones
items.summary: The Planning Commission is considering amendments to zoning and building codes designed to implement retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. This could affect how businesses operate and develop in commercial areas throughout Palo Alto.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area plan examining land use and transportation options
items.summary: Planners are studying alternative land uses and transportation approaches for the San Antonio Road area. This study could shape how that corridor develops and how residents and commuters move through it in coming years.

items.headline: Campbell updates beekeeping rules with new ordinance
items.summary: The City Council adopted an updated beekeeping ordinance that revises regulations for residential beekeeping in Campbell. The changes affect residents interested in keeping bees on their properties.
